candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above EBIT and therefore likely not embedded in 'Raw materials and services'; however it is a company-wide operating D&A total, not clearly specific to COGS, so adding it to COGS would risk misclassification/double counting versus SG&A/R&D buckets.
Component of the total D&A line, but not labeled as COGS-specific. Since a higher-level summary row exists, this should not be used separately.
Component of total D&A and not labeled as COGS-specific; also likely overlaps with R&D/intangible amortisation. Summary row exists, so avoid using component.

variables.notes: No COGS-specific depreciation/amortisation row is shown. The only explicit D&A lines are aggregate operating totals at the same level as other operating expenses, so they cannot be cleanly assigned only to the selected COGS bucket ('Raw materials and services'). Conservative choice is to add nothing separately to COGS.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above EBIT and therefore likely outside the selected SG&A rows; however it is an aggregate operating D&A total spanning more than SG&A alone and may also cover COGS and R&D-related amortisation, so adding it to XSGA would risk double counting/misallocation.
Component of total operating D&A, not clearly SG&A-specific. A summary row exists, so component should not be selected separately.

Component of total D&A and may include development-related amortisation connected to R&D. Because R&D Expenditure is already separately selected for XRD and no SG&A-specific amortisation row is shown, adding this would be risky.

variables.notes: No SG&A-specific D&A row is visible. The statement shows only aggregate operating D&A, while selected XSGA rows already include multiple overhead buckets and XRD is separately selected. To avoid misallocating total operating D&A across SG&A/COGS/R&D and to avoid double counting, do not add any separate XSGA_DA row.
